name: Research Science Institute

cost.notes: Completely FREE — free tuition, room, and board; merit-based stipend

parent_org: RSI / Center for Excellence in Education (CEE) / MIT

prior_knowledge: Exceptional academic record in science and math
Prior research experience preferred but not required

essays.prompt: Describe a scientific problem or area that interests you and explain why.
tips: Show depth of interest, not breadth
RSI values students who understand the nature of scientific inquiry

outcomes.college_impact: One of the single strongest admissions signals for MIT — RSI alumni make up a disproportionate share of MIT admits among summer program participants. Also highly regarded at Harvard, Stanford, Caltech.
outcomes.typical_results: The vast majority of RSI alumni attend top-10 universities, with MIT being the most common destination. Many publish peer-reviewed papers.

what_they_look_for: Academic excellence across all subjects, not just STEM
Maturity and ability to work independently
Genuine passion for science and research

blurbs.short: A 6-week free, fully-funded research program at MIT for rising high school seniors — the gold standard of high school research programs.
blurbs.long: RSI is arguably the most prestigious free summer program for high school students in the United States. Run by the Center for Excellence in Education (CEE) in partnership with MIT, RSI selects approximately 80 students each year from a global applicant pool (accepting international students as well). The first three weeks consist of intensive STEM coursework taught by MIT faculty; the final three weeks are a full research internship where students work in MIT labs and produce original research. The program is completely free — tuition, room, board, and a stipend are all covered. RSI alumni have an extraordinary track record at MIT specifically — the program is widely considered the single best summer program to attend if MIT is your goal.

curriculum: 3 weeks: intensive STEM coursework with MIT faculty. 3 weeks: original research project in MIT labs. Capstone: written research paper and poster presentation.
